<a id="class-noisetexture2d"></a>

# NoiseTexture2D

**Inherits:** [Texture2D](class_texture2d.md#class-texture2d) **<** [Texture](class_texture.md#class-texture) **<** [Resource](class_resource.md#class-resource) **<** [RefCounted](class_refcounted.md#class-refcounted) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

A 2D texture filled with noise generated by a [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) object.

## Description

Uses the [FastNoiseLite](class_fastnoiselite.md#class-fastnoiselite) library or other noise generators to fill the texture data of your desired size. **NoiseTexture2D** can also generate normal map textures.

The class uses [Thread](class_thread.md#class-thread)s to generate the texture data internally, so [Texture2D.get_image()](class_texture2d.md#class-texture2d-method-get-image) may return `null` if the generation process has not completed yet. In that case, you need to wait for the texture to be generated before accessing the image and the generated byte data:

```gdscript
var texture = NoiseTexture2D.new()
texture.noise = FastNoiseLite.new()
await texture.changed
var image = texture.get_image()
var data = image.get_data()
```

## Property Descriptions

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-as-normal-map"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **as_normal_map** = `false` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-as-normal-map)

-  **set_as_normal_map**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_normal_map**()

If `true`, the resulting texture contains a normal map created from the original noise interpreted as a bump map.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-bump-strength"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **bump_strength** = `8.0` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-bump-strength)

-  **set_bump_strength**(value: [float](class_float.md#class-float))
- [float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_bump_strength**()

Strength of the bump maps used in this texture. A higher value will make the bump maps appear larger while a lower value will make them appear softer.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-color-ramp"></a>

[Gradient](class_gradient.md#class-gradient) **color_ramp** [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-color-ramp)

-  **set_color_ramp**(value: [Gradient](class_gradient.md#class-gradient))
- [Gradient](class_gradient.md#class-gradient) **get_color_ramp**()

A [Gradient](class_gradient.md#class-gradient) which is used to map the luminance of each pixel to a color value.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-generate-mipmaps"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **generate_mipmaps** = `true` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-generate-mipmaps)

-  **set_generate_mipmaps**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_generating_mipmaps**()

Determines whether mipmaps are generated for this texture. Enabling this results in less texture aliasing in the distance, at the cost of increasing memory usage by roughly 33% and making the noise texture generation take longer.

**Note:** [generate_mipmaps](#class-noisetexture2d-property-generate-mipmaps) requires mipmap filtering to be enabled on the material using the **NoiseTexture2D** to have an effect.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-height"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **height** = `512` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-height)

-  **set_height**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_height**()

Height of the generated texture (in pixels).

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-in-3d-space"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **in_3d_space** = `false` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-in-3d-space)

-  **set_in_3d_space**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_in_3d_space**()

Determines whether the noise image is calculated in 3D space. May result in reduced contrast.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-invert"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **invert** = `false` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-invert)

-  **set_invert**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **get_invert**()

If `true`, inverts the noise texture. White becomes black, black becomes white.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-noise"></a>

[Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) **noise** [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-noise)

-  **set_noise**(value: [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise))
- [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) **get_noise**()

The instance of the [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) object.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-normalize"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **normalize** = `true` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-normalize)

-  **set_normalize**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_normalized**()

If `true`, the noise image coming from the noise generator is normalized to the range `0.0` to `1.0`.

Turning normalization off can affect the contrast and allows you to generate non repeating tileable noise textures.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-seamless"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **seamless** = `false` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-seamless)

-  **set_seamless**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **get_seamless**()

If `true`, a seamless texture is requested from the [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) resource.

**Note:** Seamless noise textures may take longer to generate and/or can have a lower contrast compared to non-seamless noise depending on the used [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) resource. This is because some implementations use higher dimensions for generating seamless noise.

**Note:** The default [FastNoiseLite](class_fastnoiselite.md#class-fastnoiselite) implementation uses the fallback path for seamless generation. If using a [width](#class-noisetexture2d-property-width) or [height](#class-noisetexture2d-property-height) lower than the default, you may need to increase [seamless_blend_skirt](#class-noisetexture2d-property-seamless-blend-skirt) to make seamless blending more effective.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-seamless-blend-skirt"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **seamless_blend_skirt** = `0.1` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-seamless-blend-skirt)

-  **set_seamless_blend_skirt**(value: [float](class_float.md#class-float))
- [float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_seamless_blend_skirt**()

Used for the default/fallback implementation of the seamless texture generation. It determines the distance over which the seams are blended. High values may result in less details and contrast. See [Noise](class_noise.md#class-noise) for further details.

**Note:** If using a [width](#class-noisetexture2d-property-width) or [height](#class-noisetexture2d-property-height) lower than the default, you may need to increase [seamless_blend_skirt](#class-noisetexture2d-property-seamless-blend-skirt) to make seamless blending more effective.

---

<a id="class-noisetexture2d-property-width"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **width** = `512` [🔗](#class-noisetexture2d-property-width)

-  **set_width**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_width**()

Width of the generated texture (in pixels).
